evidence_quotes.quote: When publishers delist or deactivate games,
provide access to games that are no longer commercially available.

paragraphs.text: As a concerned consumer and supporter of the Stop Killing Games initiative, I urge the Commission not to overlook the pressing issue of digital game preservation. When publishers delist or deactivate games, we as customers lose access to products we have paid for, and valuable pieces of Europes digital culture are lost forever. I call on the Commission to include clear provisions in the Digital Fairness Act to: Allow libraries, archives, and museums to preserve and provide access to games that are no longer commercially available. Require full transparency at the point of sale about the possible impermanence of digital games and platforms. Mandate that publishers enable preservationsuch as by releasing server code or removing DRMwhen support for a game ends. These steps are essential to protect my rights as a consumer, ensure fair competition, and safeguard Europes digital cultural heritage. The Stop Killing Games campaign shows there is strong public support for these changes, and I ask the Commission to address this urgent gap in the Digital Fairness Act.
